title: Blood Pressure Measurement (Palpate)
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- Altered level of consciousness.
- Suspected stroke.
- Post seizure.
- Diabetic.

#### Procedure

- Place strip into monitor. The glucometer will auto turn on.

- Select a finger and clean with an alcohol swab, allow finger to dry.

- Using a lancet, pierce the skin and squeeze a small amount of blood from the wound.

- Dispose of the used lancet appropriately.

- Place a sample of blood on the test strip until the BGL device beeps and a countdown appears.

- Apply a Band-Aid to the wound.

- Wait for the test result and record the BGL measurement.

- Dispose of the used test strip appropriately.

#### Specific Notes

- A “LO” or “HI” reading indicates a BGL <1.1mmol /L or >27.8 mmol/L.
- Presence of ^ arrow does not indicate ketones, this signals a high BGL reading. Ketone testing requires a different testing strip, this testing method will not give any indication of ketones.
